> The bar for indexing: uses current primitives correctly, represents a genuinely useful workflow someone else could pick up and adapt, and includes proof of execution. See [Example Loops](../../documentation/04%20-%20Example%20Loops/example_loops.md) for the format and existing entries.

## Plain-English Description

     the problem it solves and when you'd reach for it. -->

## Human Prompt Origin

<!-- The natural-language prompt this loop encodes.
     This is what you would have typed before you had SAILL. -->

> 

## SAILL Team Definition

<!-- Paste the complete team definition block here. -->

```
### Team Name

1. Role-One (#group) — charter.
2. Role-Two (#group, flag) — charter.
3. Role-Three (#group) — charter.
```

## Terse Invoke

<!-- One line — how a user invokes this team. -->

> "Send the [Team Name] team against [target]."

## Proof of Execution

<!-- Show that this ran. Options:
     - Output from /test-agent-teams selecting this team
     - Nonce trace showing each role executed
     - Screenshot or paste of a real run
     A submission without proof is less likely to be indexed. -->

```
<!-- paste run output here -->
```
